proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_080 (range 30-90, mean ~65) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_080 values are associated with longer progression-free survival.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import pearsonr
pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.7015 (p < 1e-300). Mean PFS rises monotonically across feature_080 quartiles: Q1=2.50, Q2=4.09, Q3=5.27, Q4=6.96 months. Univariate slope is +0.171 months per one-unit increase in feature_080.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature_063 (values 0, 1, 2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: each one-level increase is associated with shorter PFS.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
spearmanr(df['feature_063'], df['pfs_months'])
df.groupby('feature_063')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.334 (p < 1e-300). Mean PFS by level: 0 -> 5.64 (n=17592), 1 -> 4.44 (n=24971), 2 -> 3.29 (n=7437). Linear slope across levels is approximately -1.17 months per level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_056 = 0.
Patients with feature_048 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_048 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_056=1: 3.617 months (n=14969); feature_056=0: 5.153 months (n=35031). Welch t-test p < 1e-300; difference = -1.536 months. Strongly significant prognostic factor for shorter PFS.
Mean PFS feature_048=1: 3.750 (n=5090); feature_048=0: 4.800 (n=44910). Welch t-test p = 1.46e-178; difference = -1.050 months. Significant negative prognostic factor with prevalence 10%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_042 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_042 = 0 (potential treatment-like positive effect).
Patients with feature_111 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_111 = 0 (potential favorable biomarker).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_042=1: 5.408 (n=17428); feature_042=0: 4.310 (n=32572). Welch t-test p < 1e-300; difference = +1.098 months. Strong overall positive association.
Mean PFS feature_111=1: 4.865 (n=34886); feature_111=0: 4.297 (n=15114). p = 3.7e-143; difference = +0.568 months. Significant positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_015 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_015 = 0.
Patients with feature_040 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_040 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_015=1: 4.328 (n=17365); feature_015=0: 4.887 (n=32635). p = 6.07e-144; difference = -0.559 months.
Mean PFS feature_040=1: 4.317 (n=9060); feature_040=0: 4.776 (n=40940). p = 6.10e-70; difference = -0.459 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (continuous, range 0-25) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_101 (continuous, range 1-100, right-skewed) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.1155, p = 4.07e-148. Modest but very precisely estimated negative association.
Pearson r = +0.1002, p = 9.39e-112. Multivariable-adjusted slope (in OLS with top features) is +0.479 PFS-months per one-unit increase in feature_019.
Pearson r = -0.0914, p = 2.98e-93. Multivariable-adjusted slope -0.019 PFS-months per one-unit feature_101.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutually adjusting for the strongest univariate predictors (feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_067, feature_015, feature_111, feature_019, feature_101, feature_040, feature_039, feature_034), each retains an independent effect on pfs_months in a multivariable OLS model.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df[top_feats])
sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS with 13 predictors gives R^2 = 0.792. All retain significance except feature_039 (coef +0.014, p=0.32) and feature_034 (coef +0.002, p=0.88) — both univariate signals collapse after adjustment, consistent with confounding rather than independent effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039's univariate association with pfs_months is fully explained by its high correlation with feature_111: feature_039 has no independent effect after adjustment for feature_111.

analyses.result_summary: Correlation between feature_039 and feature_111 is r = +0.669 (strong). Univariate effect of feature_039 was +0.362 mo (p=5.8e-59); adjusted for feature_111, feature_039 coefficient drops to +0.014 (p=0.32). Confounding confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between feature_042 and feature_111 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_042 is greater (more positive) when feature_111 = 1 than when feature_111 = 0.

analyses.code: stratified means + interaction term in OLS adjusted for top main effects
analyses.result_summary: Stratified estimates: in feature_111=0 patients, feature_042 effect on PFS is -0.017 months (no benefit). In feature_111=1 patients, feature_042 effect is +1.577 months. Interaction coefficient in adjusted OLS: +1.559 (p < 1e-300). Highly significant positive interaction — feature_042 only confers a benefit when feature_111 is positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between feature_042 and feature_015 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_042 is reduced (or abolished) when feature_015 = 1.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified estimates: in feature_015=0 patients, feature_042 effect is +1.659 months. In feature_015=1 patients, feature_042 effect is +0.036 months (no benefit). Interaction coefficient = -1.657 (p < 1e-300). Strong antagonistic interaction — feature_015 nullifies the feature_042 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between feature_042 and feature_040 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_042 is reduced when feature_040 = 1.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified estimates: in feature_040=0 patients, feature_042 effect is +1.350 months. In feature_040=1 patients, feature_042 effect is -0.014 months. Interaction coefficient = -1.323 (p < 1e-300). feature_040 nullifies the feature_042 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refining the interaction findings: feature_042's benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in the three-way subgroup defined by feature_111=1 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0; outside this subgroup the benefit is essentially zero.

analyses.code: for combinations of (feature_111, feature_015, feature_040): mean PFS by feature_042
analyses.result_summary: In the feature_111=1 / feature_015=0 / feature_040=0 subgroup (n=18,681), feature_042 effect is +2.908 months (7.21 vs 4.30). In all 7 other subgroups the absolute effect is <= 0.13 months and not clinically meaningful. Three-way conditional pattern strongly supports a biomarker-defined responder profile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111 and feature_015 have a negative interaction on pfs_months: the favorable effect of feature_111 is reduced when feature_015 = 1.
feature_015 and feature_040 have a positive interaction on pfs_months (the negative effect of feature_015 is attenuated when feature_040 is also positive, suggesting overlap in their pathways).
feature_111 and feature_040 have a negative interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_111*feature_015 interaction adjusted for top main effects
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.834 (p = 2.8e-278). Adjusted; highly significant negative interaction.
Interaction coefficient = +0.734 (p = 1.4e-149). The negative main effects of feature_015 and feature_040 are sub-additive when both are present.
Interaction coefficient = -0.665 (p = 2.1e-113). Both modify each other's effect substantially.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_080 modifies the negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months: at higher feature_080 values, the deficit associated with feature_056=1 is larger.
feature_080 modifies the negative effect of feature_048: deficit is larger at higher feature_080 values.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_080*feature_056 interaction adjusted for top main effects
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.0106 per unit feature_080 (p = 1.9e-21). Across the feature_080 range (~60 units), this implies the feature_056 deficit grows by an additional ~0.64 months at the high end. Significant but modest in magnitude.
Interaction coefficient = -0.0064 per unit feature_080 (p = 1.6e-4). Same direction as h21; smaller in magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-like feature_042's benefit on pfs_months does NOT differ across continuous feature_080 strata (no treatment-by-feature_080 interaction).
Treatment-like feature_042's benefit on pfs_months does NOT differ across feature_063 strata (no treatment-by-feature_063 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.00163 per unit (p = 0.13). Not statistically significant — feature_042's effect is approximately constant across the feature_080 range.
Interaction coefficient = -0.0176 (p = 0.26). Not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063's negative effect interacts with feature_056: the deficit per feature_063 level is smaller (less negative) when feature_056 = 1, consistent with sub-additive effects of two negative prognostic factors.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.067 (p = 4.6e-5). Statistically significant but small in magnitude relative to the main effects (~-1.17 and -1.54 respectively). Slight sub-additivity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The mean pfs_months differs by self-reported race (feature_011: white, hispanic, black, asian, other).
The mean pfs_months differs by insurance type (feature_089: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import f_oneway
f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_011']==r,'pfs_months'] for r in df['feature_011'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Group means range narrowly from 4.679 (white) to 4.762 (other); ANOVA p > 0.1 (max - min difference 0.083 mo). No clinically or statistically meaningful disparity by race in this cohort.
Group means range from 4.685 (private) to 4.733 (uninsured); max - min difference 0.048 mo. ANOVA p > 0.5. No meaningful disparity by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_042 (the strongest treatment-like positive predictor) does not differ across self-reported race or insurance subgroups.

analyses.code: stratified mean differences of feature_042 by feature_011 and feature_089
analyses.result_summary: By race: feature_042 effect is +1.107 (white), +1.062 (asian), +1.060 (black), +1.115 (hispanic), +1.007 (other). By insurance: +1.081 (medicaid), +1.091 (private), +1.110 (medicare), +1.097 (uninsured). All within ~0.1 months of each other — no evidence of differential benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The multi-level integer features feature_021, feature_024, feature_016, feature_127, feature_001, and feature_073 (all 3-11 levels) show no monotonic association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: spearmanr for each feature with pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho values: feature_021 +0.0055 (p=0.22), feature_024 -0.0012 (p=0.78), feature_016 -0.0000 (p=0.997), feature_127 +0.0033 (p=0.46), feature_001 -0.0037 (p=0.41), feature_073 +0.0025 (p=0.57). All trivial in magnitude; none significant. Hypothesis supported: these features carry no signal for PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_034's univariate association with pfs_months (mild positive) is fully attenuated after adjustment for the rest of the top predictors.

analyses.code: compare univariate t-test vs adjusted OLS coefficient for feature_034
analyses.result_summary: Univariate effect of feature_034 = +0.114 (p=1.04e-6). After adjustment for the top 12 predictors, coefficient drops to +0.002 (p=0.88). Confounding confirmed; feature_034 is correlated with feature_040 (r=-0.36).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond feature_111, feature_015, and feature_040, no other binary feature in the dataset has a clinically meaningful interaction with feature_042 (i.e., interaction coefficient |>=0.5 months| after adjusting for the established main effects).

analyses.code: for each of the ~80 binary features b: fit OLS with feature_042 main effect + b main effect + interaction adjusted for top 11 predictors; rank by interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: After ranking the ~80 binary features by interaction strength with feature_042: only feature_111 (+1.56), feature_039 (+1.03 — collinear with feature_111), feature_040 (-1.34), feature_015 (-1.66), and feature_034 (+0.38) reach interaction coefficients above 0.30 in absolute value. All others have interaction coefficients below 0.20 mo. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_042 × feature_034 interaction is real but modest: feature_042's benefit is approximately 0.4 months larger when feature_034 = 1, particularly within the feature_111+ subgroup where feature_042 is already effective.

analyses.code: stratified means: feature_042 effect by feature_111 x feature_034
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect: in feature_111=0/feature_034=0 subgroup +0.001 (n=9540); 111=0/034=1 -0.053 (n=5574); 111=1/034=0 +1.358 (n=21917); 111=1/034=1 +1.944 (n=12969). Interaction is concentrated in feature_111+ patients: feature_034=1 amplifies the feature_042 benefit by ~0.6 months on top of the already-large effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_056 and feature_048 act as additive negative prognostic factors with no meaningful interaction with feature_042 (their effects are independent of treatment-like response).

analyses.code: stratified means: feature_042 effect by feature_056 and by feature_048
analyses.result_summary: Stratified by feature_056: effect of feature_042 is +1.096 (056=0) vs +1.102 (056=1) — virtually identical. Stratified by feature_048: effect is +1.099 (048=0) vs +1.058 (048=1) — also virtually identical. Multivariable interaction coefficients are not significant. Hypothesis supported: feature_056 and feature_048 are pure prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model that includes the 11 main effects plus the six pairwise interactions among (feature_042, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040) plus the two age-like prognostic interactions (feature_080×feature_056, feature_080×feature_048) explains substantially more variance in pfs_months than the main-effects-only model.

analyses.code: compare R^2 of main-effects model vs interaction-augmented model
analyses.result_summary: Main-effects-only R^2 = 0.792. Adding the 8 named interaction terms raises R^2 to 0.854 (residual std 0.955 vs outcome std 2.496). The eight interactions jointly explain ~6 percentage points of additional variance — substantial.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final synthesis: the data are consistent with feature_042 being a targeted-therapy-like intervention whose benefit depends on a biomarker profile (feature_111 positive, feature_015 negative, feature_040 negative); feature_080 and feature_063 are dominant continuous/ordinal prognostic factors; feature_056 and feature_048 are independent negative prognostic factors; and self-reported race (feature_011) and insurance (feature_089) show no detectable association with pfs_months either as main effects or as modifiers of feature_042 response.

analyses.code: synthesis from all prior analyses; confirm with stratified inspection
analyses.result_summary: All prior findings are consistent with this synthesis. The single largest predictive interaction in the dataset is feature_042 × feature_111 (+1.55 mo), with antagonistic interactions of feature_042 with feature_015 (-1.66) and feature_040 (-1.32). The largest prognostic main effects are feature_080 (+0.18/unit, ~10 mo across full range), feature_063 (-1.17/level), feature_056 (-1.54), and feature_048 (-1.05). Demographics show no association. Final adjusted model with main effects + key interactions: R^2 = 0.854.
